expression: Alla goda ting är tre
literalTranslation: All good things are three
meaning: A third success or attempt often completes the pattern and feels especially satisfying.
usageNote: Use it lightheartedly after two similar good results when a third one arrives or when you want to encourage one more try.
exampleSentence: Två avtal är redan klara, och nu kom det tredje också, alla goda ting är tre.
exampleTranslation: Two deals are already done, and now the third one came through as well; good things come in threes.

expression: Alla katter är grå i mörkret
literalTranslation: All cats are gray in the dark
meaning: When conditions are unclear, important differences are hard to see.
usageNote: Use it when details blur together and people are judging without enough light, context, or precision.
exampleSentence: På den där suddiga videolänken är alla katter grå i mörkret.
exampleTranslation: On that blurry video call, all cats are gray in the dark.

expression: Alla känner apan, apan känner ingen
literalTranslation: Everyone knows the monkey, the monkey knows no one
meaning: A well-known person is recognized by many strangers, even though they do not know those people back.
usageNote: Use it about public visibility, local fame, or the odd imbalance between being known and actually knowing others.
exampleSentence: Sedan serien släpptes känns det som att alla känner apan, apan känner ingen.
exampleTranslation: Since the series came out, it feels like everyone knows the monkey while the monkey knows no one.

expression: Alla vägar bär till Rom
literalTranslation: All roads lead to Rome
meaning: Different methods can still lead to the same end result.
usageNote: Use it when several approaches are valid and there is no single correct route to the goal.
exampleSentence: Vi löser det på olika sätt, men alla vägar bär till Rom.
exampleTranslation: We solve it in different ways, but all roads lead to Rome.

expression: Alla är vi barn i början
literalTranslation: We are all children at the beginning
meaning: Everyone starts out inexperienced and has to learn from the beginning.
usageNote: Use it kindly when someone is new, awkward, or still learning and does not deserve harsh judgment.
exampleSentence: Låt henne få lite tid, alla är vi barn i början.
exampleTranslation: Give her a little time; we all start out as beginners.

expression: Anfall är bästa försvar
literalTranslation: Attack is the best defense
meaning: Taking the initiative can protect you better than waiting passively.
usageNote: Use it when a bold, proactive move is safer or stronger than staying purely defensive.
exampleSentence: Om kritiken ändå kommer kan vi lika gärna gå ut först, anfall är bästa försvar.
exampleTranslation: If the criticism is coming anyway, we may as well speak first; attack is the best defense.

expression: Arga katter får rivet skinn
literalTranslation: Angry cats get scratched skin
meaning: People who lash out at others often end up hurting themselves in the process.
usageNote: Use it as a warning when someone is acting aggressively and is likely to suffer for it later.
exampleSentence: Han startar bråk med alla och blir förvånad över motståndet, arga katter får rivet skinn.
exampleTranslation: He picks fights with everyone and is surprised by the backlash; angry cats get scratched skin.

expression: Allt är inte guld som glimmar
literalTranslation: Not everything that glitters is gold
meaning: Something attractive on the surface may still be disappointing or deceptive underneath.
usageNote: Use it when warning someone not to mistake polish, status, or hype for real value.
exampleSentence: Erbjudandet låter exklusivt, men allt är inte guld som glimmar.
exampleTranslation: The offer sounds exclusive, but not everything that glitters is gold.

expression: Att skiljas är att dö en smula
literalTranslation: To part is to die a little
meaning: Saying goodbye to someone important is genuinely painful.
usageNote: Use it when a separation feels emotionally heavy, not just practical or temporary.
exampleSentence: När barnen flyttade ut kändes det verkligen som att skiljas är att dö en smula.
exampleTranslation: When the children moved out, it really felt true that to part is to die a little.

expression: Bra karl reder sig själv
literalTranslation: A capable man manages on his own
meaning: A resourceful person should not be too dependent on others.
usageNote: Use it about self-reliance, though the wording sounds traditional and is often quoted with a slightly old-fashioned tone.
exampleSentence: Han ville inte ringa efter hjälp direkt, bra karl reder sig själv, sa han.
exampleTranslation: He did not want to call for help right away; a capable person manages on their own, he said.

expression: Bättre lyss till den sträng som brast än aldrig spänna en båge
literalTranslation: Better to listen to the string that snapped than never bend a bow
meaning: It is better to risk failure in a brave attempt than never dare to try at all.
usageNote: Use it to defend effort, courage, and wholehearted attempts, even when they end badly.
exampleSentence: Projektet höll inte hela vägen, men bättre lyss till den sträng som brast än aldrig spänna en båge.
exampleTranslation: The project did not hold all the way through, but it is better to risk a snapped string than never draw the bow.

expression: Bättre skelögd än blind
literalTranslation: Better cross-eyed than blind
meaning: An imperfect solution is still better than having none at all.
usageNote: Use it when something is flawed but still clearly preferable to total failure or total lack of information.
exampleSentence: Rapporten är inte perfekt, men bättre skelögd än blind.
exampleTranslation: The report is not perfect, but imperfect is better than blind.

expression: Den sig i leken ger, han måste leken tåla
literalTranslation: Whoever enters the game must tolerate the game
meaning: If you choose to join in, you also have to accept the risks, criticism, or rough edges that come with it.
usageNote: Use it when someone wants the benefits of a situation but resists the obvious consequences of being involved.
exampleSentence: Du kan inte starta debatten och sedan bli chockad av svaren, den sig i leken ger, han måste leken tåla.
exampleTranslation: You cannot start the debate and then act shocked by the replies; if you join the game, you have to take it.

expression: Den som gräver en grop åt andra faller ofta själv däri
literalTranslation: Who digs a pit for others often falls into it themselves
meaning: People who set traps for others are often caught by their own schemes.
usageNote: Use it when manipulative behavior backfires and harms the person who started it.
exampleSentence: Han försökte smutskasta sin kollega men avslöjade sig själv i stället, den som gräver en grop åt andra faller ofta själv däri.
exampleTranslation: He tried to smear his colleague but exposed himself instead; whoever digs a pit for others often falls into it.

expression: Den som söker han finner
literalTranslation: Who seeks, finds
meaning: Persistent searching and effort usually lead to results.
usageNote: Use it to encourage someone who is still looking, asking questions, or refusing to give up.
exampleSentence: Fortsätt ringa runt, den som söker han finner.
exampleTranslation: Keep calling around; whoever keeps looking will find an answer.

expression: Den som länge sover på sin bädd, han får litet för sitt näbb
literalTranslation: Who sleeps long in their bed gets little for their beak
meaning: If you stay idle too long, you should not expect much reward.
usageNote: Use it to criticize laziness or passivity when work, initiative, or timing matters.
exampleSentence: Han väntade tills allt redan var taget, den som länge sover på sin bädd får litet för sitt näbb.
exampleTranslation: He waited until everything was already taken; whoever sleeps too long gets little reward.

expression: De synder man mest ångrar på gamla dar är de man inte begått
literalTranslation: The sins one regrets most in old age are the ones one did not commit
meaning: People often regret missed experiences and missed courage more than the mistakes they actually made.
usageNote: Use it when encouraging someone not to let fear make their life smaller than it has to be.
exampleSentence: Sök jobbet ändå, de synder man mest ångrar på gamla dar är de man inte begått.
exampleTranslation: Apply for the job anyway; the things people regret most are often the risks they never took.

expression: Det dunkelt sagda är det dunkelt tänkta
literalTranslation: What is said obscurely is thought obscurely
meaning: Unclear language often reveals unclear thinking behind it.
usageNote: Use it when vague wording is hiding confusion, weak reasoning, or ideas that are not finished yet.
exampleSentence: Om ingen förstår planen kanske problemet sitter i själva planen, det dunkelt sagda är det dunkelt tänkta.
exampleTranslation: If nobody understands the plan, the problem may be the plan itself; what is said obscurely is thought obscurely.

expression: Det hushåll är ej utan kval, där hanen kacklar och hönan gal
literalTranslation: That household is not without trouble where the rooster clucks and the hen crows
meaning: The saying claims that disorder follows when expected roles are reversed.
usageNote: Use it cautiously, if at all: the wording is old-fashioned and sexist, so today it is mainly quoted ironically or as a historical proverb.
exampleSentence: Hon använde ordspråket ironiskt, just för att visa hur daterat det låter: det hushåll är ej utan kval, där hanen kacklar och hönan gal.
exampleTranslation: She used the proverb ironically to show how dated it sounds: that household is not without trouble where the rooster clucks and the hen crows.

expression: Det är en ond fågel, som orent gör i sitt rede
literalTranslation: It is an evil bird that fouls its own nest
meaning: Someone who harms their own home, group, or people is acting disloyally.
usageNote: Use it when a person damages the very environment they depend on or belong to.
exampleSentence: Att medvetet sabotera sitt eget lag är verkligen att göra orent i sitt rede.
exampleTranslation: Deliberately sabotaging your own team is truly fouling your own nest.

expression: Det är ingen ko på isen
literalTranslation: There is no cow on the ice
meaning: There is no real emergency yet, so there is no need to panic.
usageNote: Use it to calm someone down when a situation is still manageable and there is time to act.
exampleSentence: Vi ligger efter, men det är ingen ko på isen än.
exampleTranslation: We are behind schedule, but it is not a real crisis yet.

expression: Det är mänskligt att fela
literalTranslation: It is human to err
meaning: Making mistakes is a natural part of being human.
usageNote: Use it to show grace after an error, especially when shame is less useful than repair and perspective.
exampleSentence: Det blev fel i utskicket, men det är mänskligt att fela.
exampleTranslation: There was a mistake in the mailing, but to err is human.

expression: Det är saligare att giva än att taga
literalTranslation: It is more blessed to give than to take
meaning: Giving can be more fulfilling than receiving.
usageNote: Use it when generosity, kindness, or shared abundance matters more than personal gain.
exampleSentence: Hon gav bort bonusen till insamlingen och sa att det är saligare att giva än att taga.
exampleTranslation: She donated the bonus to the fundraiser and said it is more blessed to give than to receive.

expression: Det är svårt lära gammal hund sitta
literalTranslation: It is hard to teach an old dog to sit
meaning: Long-established habits are difficult to change.
usageNote: Use it when someone with fixed routines resists new methods, tools, or expectations.
exampleSentence: Han har bokfört på samma sätt i trettio år, det är svårt lära gammal hund sitta.
exampleTranslation: He has done the bookkeeping the same way for thirty years; it is hard to teach an old dog new tricks.

expression: Envar sin egen lyckas smed
literalTranslation: Each person is the smith of their own fortune
meaning: People shape much of their own future through their choices and effort.
usageNote: Use it when stressing personal responsibility, initiative, and the role of action in changing one's life.
exampleSentence: Ingen annan kommer bygga hennes karriär åt henne, envar sin egen lyckas smed.
exampleTranslation: No one else is going to build her career for her; each person is the smith of their own fortune.

expression: Ett mjukt svar stillar vreden
literalTranslation: A gentle answer calms anger
meaning: A calm and kind response can reduce someone else's anger.
usageNote: Use it when de-escalation matters more than winning the moment or answering sharply.
exampleSentence: Hon svarade lugnt trots utskällningen, ett mjukt svar stillar vreden.
exampleTranslation: She answered calmly despite the scolding; a gentle answer calms anger.

expression: En svala gör ingen sommar
literalTranslation: One swallow does not make a summer
meaning: A single sign is not enough to prove a larger pattern.
usageNote: Use it when one promising event is being treated as stronger evidence than it really is.
exampleSentence: En bra vecka betyder inte att krisen är över, en svala gör ingen sommar.
exampleTranslation: One good week does not mean the crisis is over; one swallow does not make a summer.

expression: En tiggare fruktar varken tjuv eller rövare
literalTranslation: A beggar fears neither thief nor robber
meaning: Someone with nothing to lose has little to fear from being robbed.
usageNote: Use it when a person is already so stripped down that ordinary threats no longer have much power over them.
exampleSentence: Han tog risken utan att blinka, en tiggare fruktar varken tjuv eller rövare.
exampleTranslation: He took the risk without blinking; a beggar fears neither thief nor robber.

expression: Egen härd är guld värd
literalTranslation: One's own hearth is worth gold
meaning: Having a home of your own is deeply valuable.
usageNote: Use it when returning home feels better than luxury elsewhere or when praising the comfort of having one's own place.
exampleSentence: Hotellet var fint, men egen härd är guld värd.
exampleTranslation: The hotel was nice, but your own hearth is worth gold.

expression: Eget ros är elakt os
literalTranslation: Self-praise is nasty smoke
meaning: Praising yourself too much feels unpleasant and unbecoming to others.
usageNote: Use it when someone keeps advertising their own greatness instead of letting their actions speak.
exampleSentence: Låt resultaten tala lite också, eget ros är elakt os.
exampleTranslation: Let the results speak too; self-praise is unpleasant smoke.

expression: Fägring utan tukt är som ros utan lukt
literalTranslation: Beauty without discipline is like a rose without scent
meaning: Beauty alone is not enough if character, substance, or restraint is missing.
usageNote: Use it when charm or appearance looks impressive but lacks the deeper qualities that make it truly valuable.
exampleSentence: Varumärket är snyggt, men utan innehåll blir det bara fägring utan tukt.
exampleTranslation: The brand looks beautiful, but without substance it is just beauty without depth.

expression: Finns det hjärterum finns det stjärterum
literalTranslation: If there is room in the heart, there is room for the rear end
meaning: If people really care, they will make room for one more person.
usageNote: Use it warmly when including someone despite tight space, limited resources, or imperfect logistics.
exampleSentence: Kom och sitt med oss, finns det hjärterum finns det stjärterum.
exampleTranslation: Come sit with us; if there is room in the heart, there is room for one more.

expression: Gud tar och Gud ger
literalTranslation: God takes and God gives
meaning: Life contains both losses and gifts that lie beyond our control.
usageNote: Use it with a sober, accepting tone when speaking about the uneven way fortune can change.
exampleSentence: Det blev en tung vår men en oväntat fin höst, gud tar och Gud ger.
exampleTranslation: It was a hard spring but an unexpectedly good autumn; life both takes and gives.

expression: Guds vägar äro outgrundliga
literalTranslation: God's ways are unfathomable
meaning: The deeper reasons behind events may be impossible to understand in the moment.
usageNote: Use it when a difficult or puzzling turn of events seems to defy simple explanation, often with a religious or reflective tone.
exampleSentence: Ingen hade kunnat förutse att motgången skulle leda hit, Guds vägar äro outgrundliga.
exampleTranslation: No one could have predicted that the setback would lead here; God's ways are unfathomable.

expression: Guldprov i glöd, vänprov i nöd
literalTranslation: Gold is tested in heat, a friend is tested in need
meaning: You learn what something is truly worth only under pressure, especially a friendship.
usageNote: Use it when hardship reveals who is loyal, generous, and really present when it counts.
exampleSentence: Det var först under sjukskrivningen jag såg vilka som stannade kvar, guldprov i glöd, vänprov i nöd.
exampleTranslation: It was only during the sick leave that I saw who stayed; gold is tested in heat, a friend in need.

expression: Har man tagit fan i båten får man ro honom i land
literalTranslation: If you have taken the devil into the boat, you must row him ashore
meaning: Once you have started something troublesome, you still have to deal with the consequences all the way through.
usageNote: Use it when backing out is no longer realistic and the messy commitment has to be carried to the end.
exampleSentence: Vi tog in den krångliga kunden, så nu får vi ro honom i land.
exampleTranslation: We took on the complicated client, so now we have to see it through.

expression: Högmod går före fall
literalTranslation: Pride goes before a fall
meaning: Arrogance often comes right before humiliation or failure.
usageNote: Use it when someone's overconfidence is making them careless, rude, or blind to risk.
exampleSentence: Han skrattade åt konkurrenterna tills siffrorna kom, högmod går före fall.
exampleTranslation: He laughed at the competitors until the numbers came in; pride goes before a fall.

expression: Har man sagt A får man säga B
literalTranslation: If one has said A, one must say B
meaning: If you begin to reveal or commit to something, you should follow through clearly.
usageNote: Use it when someone starts a thought, promise, or admission but tries to stop before the important part.
exampleSentence: Nu när du nämnt saken får du fortsätta, har man sagt A får man säga B.
exampleTranslation: Now that you mentioned it, you have to continue; if you say A, you have to say B.

expression: Ingen är profet i sitt eget land
literalTranslation: No one is a prophet in their own land
meaning: People are often taken least seriously by those closest to them.
usageNote: Use it when outside recognition comes more easily than respect from one's own circle, hometown, or workplace.
exampleSentence: Hans föreläsningar uppskattas överallt utom på den gamla institutionen, ingen är profet i sitt eget land.
exampleTranslation: His lectures are appreciated everywhere except at the old department; no one is a prophet in their own land.

expression: Ingenting är nytt under solen
literalTranslation: Nothing is new under the sun
meaning: What looks new is often just an old human pattern in a new form.
usageNote: Use it when trends, conflicts, or ideas feel familiar despite being presented as revolutionary.
exampleSentence: Appen är ny men problemen är gamla, ingenting är nytt under solen.
exampleTranslation: The app is new, but the problems are old; nothing is new under the sun.

expression: Jorden är alltid frusen för lata svin
literalTranslation: The ground is always frozen for lazy swine
meaning: Lazy people always seem to find an excuse for not getting started.
usageNote: Use it only in rough or joking contexts, since the wording is blunt and openly insulting.
exampleSentence: Han hittade ett nytt skäl varje vecka att skjuta upp jobbet, jorden är alltid frusen för lata svin.
exampleTranslation: He found a new reason every week to postpone the work; the ground is always frozen for lazy swine.

expression: Kasta inte yxan i sjön
literalTranslation: Do not throw the axe into the lake
meaning: Do not give up just because the situation suddenly looks difficult.
usageNote: Use it to stop someone from abandoning an effort too early, especially when a solution may still exist.
exampleSentence: Vi har inte provat den sista vägen än, kasta inte yxan i sjön.
exampleTranslation: We have not tried the last option yet; do not throw the axe into the lake.

expression: Kasta inte pärlor åt svinen
literalTranslation: Do not throw pearls to swine
meaning: Do not waste something valuable on people who will not understand or appreciate it.
usageNote: Use it when time, beauty, care, or effort is being spent on an audience that has no use for it.
exampleSentence: Spara den presentationen till kunder som faktiskt bryr sig, kasta inte pärlor åt svinen.
exampleTranslation: Save that presentation for clients who actually care; do not throw pearls to swine.

expression: Kasta inte sten i glashus
literalTranslation: Do not throw stones in a glass house
meaning: Do not attack others for faults that you share yourself.
usageNote: Use it when criticism sounds hypocritical because the speaker is vulnerable in the same way.
exampleSentence: Han klagar på andras sena svar men missar själv varje deadline, kasta inte sten i glashus.
exampleTranslation: He complains about other people's late replies but misses every deadline himself; do not throw stones in a glass house.

expression: Lat mans bön blir sällan hörd
literalTranslation: A lazy man's prayer is seldom heard
meaning: Wishing for results without effort rarely helps.
usageNote: Use it when someone wants the outcome but keeps refusing the work that would make it possible.
exampleSentence: Att hoppas på bättre siffror utan att ändra något är som att tro att lat mans bön blir hörd.
exampleTranslation: Hoping for better numbers without changing anything is like believing a lazy man's prayer will be heard.

expression: Lärdom är mer värt än guld
literalTranslation: Learning is worth more than gold
meaning: Knowledge and wisdom have deeper value than money alone.
usageNote: Use it when defending study, insight, or long-term understanding over short-term material gain.
exampleSentence: Hon tackade nej till den snabba affären, lärdom är mer värt än guld på sikt.
exampleTranslation: She turned down the quick deal; in the long run, learning is worth more than gold.

expression: Man skall ej klå där som det ej kliar
literalTranslation: One should not scratch where it does not itch
meaning: Do not create problems by poking at something that was not troubling anyone.
usageNote: Use it when needless meddling risks turning a quiet situation into a complicated one.
exampleSentence: Systemet fungerar ju, vi behöver inte riva upp allt nu, man skall ej klå där som det ej kliar.
exampleTranslation: The system is working, so we do not need to tear everything up now; do not scratch where it does not itch.

expression: Nya kvastar sopar bäst
literalTranslation: New brooms sweep best
meaning: New people often bring fresh energy and make a strong early impression.
usageNote: Use it when someone new throws themselves into a task with extra visible force or enthusiasm.
exampleSentence: Den nya chefen ändrade rutiner första veckan, nya kvastar sopar bäst.
exampleTranslation: The new manager changed routines in the first week; new brooms sweep best.

expression: När det regnar manna har den fattige ingen sked
literalTranslation: When manna rains down, the poor person has no spoon
meaning: Opportunity can appear, yet the people who need it most may still lack the means to benefit from it.
usageNote: Use it when access, timing, or structural disadvantage prevents someone from making use of good fortune.
exampleSentence: Stipendiet var öppet för alla, men ansökan krävde resurser många saknade, när det regnar manna har den fattige ingen sked.
exampleTranslation: The grant was open to everyone, but the application required resources many lacked; when manna falls, the poor person has no spoon.

expression: När katten är borta dansar råttorna på bordet
literalTranslation: When the cat is away, the rats dance on the table
meaning: Without supervision, people often misbehave or let order collapse.
usageNote: Use it when authority disappears and everyone immediately becomes bolder, looser, or messier.
exampleSentence: Så fort läraren gick ut blev det liv i klassrummet, när katten är borta dansar råttorna på bordet.
exampleTranslation: As soon as the teacher left, the classroom became noisy; when the cat is away, the rats dance on the table.

expression: När man talar om trollen så står de i farstun
literalTranslation: When one speaks of the trolls, they are standing in the entryway
meaning: The person you have just been talking about suddenly appears.
usageNote: Use it playfully when someone turns up at the exact moment they were being mentioned.
exampleSentence: Vi pratade just om dig, när man talar om trollen så står de i farstun.
exampleTranslation: We were just talking about you; speak of the devil and they appear.

expression: Ont är ej gott, förrän värre kommer
literalTranslation: Bad is not good until worse arrives
meaning: Something painful can seem almost acceptable once an even worse alternative appears.
usageNote: Use it when perspective shifts and yesterday's problem suddenly looks mild compared with today's.
exampleSentence: Servern var långsam i går men nu är allt nere, ont är ej gott förrän värre kommer.
exampleTranslation: The server was slow yesterday, but now everything is down; bad does not seem mild until worse arrives.

expression: Ofta ligger ormen under rosenbusken
literalTranslation: Often the snake lies under the rose bush
meaning: Danger or trouble can hide beneath something that looks harmless or attractive.
usageNote: Use it when a pleasant surface is making people overlook an obvious hidden risk.
exampleSentence: Affären såg för bra ut för att vara sann, ofta ligger ormen under rosenbusken.
exampleTranslation: The deal looked too good to be true; danger often hides under the rose bush.

expression: Ont ska med ont fördrivas
literalTranslation: Evil should be driven out with evil
meaning: Sometimes one unpleasant measure is used to counter another problem.
usageNote: Use it about harsh remedies or rough countermeasures, usually with a resigned tone rather than admiration.
exampleSentence: Det blev ännu en dyr tillfällig lösning för att stoppa krisen, ont ska med ont fördrivas.
exampleTranslation: It took another costly temporary fix to stop the crisis; one harsh problem is being driven out by another.

expression: Rom byggdes inte på en dag
literalTranslation: Rome was not built in a day
meaning: Important work takes time and cannot be rushed into existence overnight.
usageNote: Use it to calm unrealistic urgency and remind people that solid results usually grow step by step.
exampleSentence: Vi har bara jobbat med plattformen i två veckor, Rom byggdes inte på en dag.
exampleTranslation: We have only worked on the platform for two weeks; Rome was not built in a day.

expression: Sila mygg och svälja kameler
literalTranslation: To strain gnats and swallow camels
meaning: To be strict about tiny faults while ignoring much bigger ones.
usageNote: Use it when attention to trivia is crowding out judgment about what actually matters.
exampleSentence: De fastnade i färgen på rubriken men missade dataläckan, de silar mygg och sväljer kameler.
exampleTranslation: They got stuck on the heading color but missed the data leak; they strain gnats and swallow camels.

expression: Som man sår får man skörda
literalTranslation: As one sows, one reaps
meaning: The consequences you face tend to reflect the actions you chose earlier.
usageNote: Use it when results, good or bad, clearly grow out of what someone has been doing over time.
exampleSentence: Han behandlade folk illa i flera år och blev ensam till slut, som man sår får man skörda.
exampleTranslation: He treated people badly for years and ended up alone; as one sows, one reaps.

expression: Stor i orden, liten på jorden
literalTranslation: Big in words, small on earth
meaning: Someone talks grandly about themselves but has little real substance to show for it.
usageNote: Use it when bragging is far bigger than performance, competence, or character.
exampleSentence: Han lovade revolution men levererade knappt en skiss, stor i orden, liten på jorden.
exampleTranslation: He promised a revolution but delivered barely a sketch; big in words, small in substance.

expression: Synden straffar sig själv
literalTranslation: The sin punishes itself
meaning: Wrongdoing often creates its own punishment through the consequences it sets in motion.
usageNote: Use it when bad choices rebound naturally, even without anyone planning revenge.
exampleSentence: Han ljög för att vinna tid men förlorade allas förtroende, synden straffar sig själv.
exampleTranslation: He lied to buy time but lost everyone's trust; wrongdoing punishes itself.

expression: Surt, sa räven om rönnbären
literalTranslation: Sour, said the fox about the rowan berries
meaning: People often dismiss what they cannot get by pretending they never wanted it anyway.
usageNote: Use it when disappointment gets repackaged as contempt to protect someone's pride.
exampleSentence: Han kallade priset ointressant först efter att han förlorade budgivningen, surt, sa räven om rönnbären.
exampleTranslation: He called the prize unimportant only after losing the bidding; sour grapes, said the fox.

expression: Sent skall syndaren vakna
literalTranslation: Late shall the sinner awaken
meaning: Some people realize their mistake only after it is far too late to avoid the damage.
usageNote: Use it when insight comes with painful delay and the chance to act earlier is already gone.
exampleSentence: Nu när kontraktet är skrivet börjar de ställa rätt frågor, sent skall syndaren vakna.
exampleTranslation: Now that the contract is signed, they are finally asking the right questions; the sinner wakes late.

expression: Som man bäddar får man ligga
literalTranslation: As one makes the bed, one must lie in it
meaning: You have to live with the consequences of the situation you created.
usageNote: Use it when someone's current discomfort clearly follows from earlier choices they made themselves.
exampleSentence: Han ignorerade alla varningar och nu får han ta följderna, som man bäddar får man ligga.
exampleTranslation: He ignored every warning and now has to take the consequences; as you make your bed, so you must lie in it.

expression: Tala med bönder på bönders vis och med de lärde på latin
literalTranslation: Speak with farmers in the farmers' way and with the learned in Latin
meaning: Adjust your language to the knowledge, background, and expectations of the people you are talking to.
usageNote: Use it when good communication depends on meeting the audience where they are instead of showing off.
exampleSentence: Vi måste skriva om guiden för nya användare, tala med bönder på bönders vis och med de lärde på latin.
exampleTranslation: We need to rewrite the guide for new users; speak to people in the language they can actually use.

expression: Tala är silver, tiga är guld
literalTranslation: Speech is silver, silence is gold
meaning: Silence can be wiser and more valuable than speaking.
usageNote: Use it when restraint, listening, or discretion matters more than filling the space with words.
exampleSentence: Alla känslor behöver inte sägas direkt, tala är silver, tiga är guld.
exampleTranslation: Not every feeling has to be spoken immediately; speech is silver, silence is gold.

expression: Tron kan försätta berg
literalTranslation: Faith can move mountains
meaning: Deep conviction can make seemingly impossible things achievable.
usageNote: Use it when confidence, belief, or persistence is what keeps a difficult effort alive.
exampleSentence: Ingen trodde att byn skulle samla ihop pengarna, men tron kan försätta berg.
exampleTranslation: No one believed the village would raise the money, but faith can move mountains.

expression: Var dag har nog av sin egen plåga
literalTranslation: Each day has enough of its own trouble
meaning: There is no need to add tomorrow's worries to today's burdens.
usageNote: Use it when someone is carrying more anxiety than the present moment already requires.
exampleSentence: Låt oss lösa dagens problem först, var dag har nog av sin egen plåga.
exampleTranslation: Let us solve today's problems first; each day has enough trouble of its own.

expression: Var och en får bli salig på sin fason
literalTranslation: Each person may be saved in their own manner
meaning: People are entitled to their own way of living or believing, as long as it does not harm others.
usageNote: Use it when you want to signal tolerance rather than control over how others choose to do things.
exampleSentence: Jag hade inte valt så själv, men var och en får bli salig på sin fason.
exampleTranslation: I would not have chosen that myself, but everyone may follow their own path.

expression: Var och en är sin egen lyckas smed
literalTranslation: Each person is the smith of their own fortune
meaning: You carry a real share of responsibility for shaping your own life and success.
usageNote: Use it when emphasizing agency, discipline, and the idea that waiting passively will not change much.
exampleSentence: Han slutade vänta på rätt tillfälle och skapade ett själv, var och en är sin egen lyckas smed.
exampleTranslation: He stopped waiting for the right moment and created one himself; each person is the smith of their own fortune.

expression: Var sak har sin tid
literalTranslation: Every thing has its time
meaning: Some things cannot be forced before their proper moment arrives.
usageNote: Use it when patience and timing matter more than pushing harder right now.
exampleSentence: Vi kan inte fatta det beslutet i panik, var sak har sin tid.
exampleTranslation: We cannot make that decision in panic; everything has its proper time.

expression: Varav hjärtat är fullt talar munnen
literalTranslation: What the heart is full of, the mouth speaks
meaning: What matters most to a person eventually shows up in what they say.
usageNote: Use it when someone's repeated words reveal their true values, worries, or desires better than their polished image does.
exampleSentence: Han nämner pengar i varje samtal, varav hjärtat är fullt talar munnen.
exampleTranslation: He mentions money in every conversation; what the heart is full of, the mouth speaks.

expression: Villiga oxar skall man ej jäkta
literalTranslation: Willing oxen should not be rushed
meaning: People who are already cooperative do not need extra pressure.
usageNote: Use it when someone helpful is being pushed unnecessarily hard and the pressure risks making things worse.
exampleSentence: Teamet jobbar redan kvällar, villiga oxar skall man ej jäkta.
exampleTranslation: The team is already working evenings; willing oxen should not be rushed.

expression: Vänd den andra kinden till
literalTranslation: Turn the other cheek
meaning: Do not answer hurt with retaliation.
usageNote: Use it when restraint, dignity, or peace matters more than getting even.
exampleSentence: Hon valde att inte svara på förolämpningen utan vände den andra kinden till.
exampleTranslation: She chose not to answer the insult and turned the other cheek.

expression: Även dåren anses vis om han tiger
literalTranslation: Even the fool is considered wise if he keeps silent
meaning: Silence can conceal foolishness that speech would reveal immediately.
usageNote: Use it when someone would be wiser to listen than to expose their confusion by talking too soon.
exampleSentence: Ibland hade mötet tjänat på mer tystnad, även dåren anses vis om han tiger.
exampleTranslation: Sometimes the meeting would have benefited from more silence; even the fool seems wise when silent.

expression: Öga för öga, tand för tand
literalTranslation: Eye for eye, tooth for tooth
meaning: A punishment or revenge should mirror the harm that was done.
usageNote: Use it when describing strict retaliation or a payback mindset, usually more as observation than advice.
exampleSentence: Debatten blev snabbt ett öga för öga, tand för tand mellan de två lägren.
exampleTranslation: The debate quickly turned into an eye-for-an-eye struggle between the two camps.

expression: Östanväder och kvinnoträta börjar med storm och slutar med väta
literalTranslation: East wind and women's quarrels begin with storm and end with wetness
meaning: The proverb compares quarrels to bad weather that starts violently and ends in tears.
usageNote: Use it cautiously, if at all: the saying is strongly dated and sexist, so today it is mostly cited historically or with clear irony.
exampleSentence: Han nämnde ordspråket bara för att kritisera det som ett gammalt synsätt: östanväder och kvinnoträta börjar med storm och slutar med väta.
exampleTranslation: He mentioned the proverb only to criticize it as an old attitude: east wind and women's quarrels begin with storm and end with tears.
